title: Extract Top Keywords by Frequency
description: Extract the most frequent meaningful words from a document for tagging or search indexing.
problem: Write top_keywords(text, n, stopwords) that lowercases text, splits on non-alphanumeric characters, removes words in `stopwords` and empty strings, then returns the top n words by frequency. Ties broken by first appearance order.
starter_code:
import re
from collections import Counter

def top_keywords(text, n, stopwords):
    # Tokenize, remove stopwords, return top n by frequency (ties: first-seen order)
    return []
hint: words = re.findall(r'[a-z0-9]+', text.lower()); filter out stopwords; use Counter, then sort by (-count, first_index).

test_cases.label: Basic extraction
test_cases.call: top_keywords('The cat is a cat. The dog is a dog.', 2, stopwords)
